steps.title: Use the discriminant
steps.content: For a quadratic equation, the sign of the discriminant tells how many distinct real solutions there are. Here $a=2$, $b=-10$, and $c=8$.

steps.title: Calculate the discriminant
steps.content: Compute $$ b^2-4ac=(-10)^2-4(2)(8)=100-64=36 $$ Since $36>0$, the equation has exactly two distinct real solutions. The answer is B.
